2605.29648.title: Verifiable Rewards Beyond Math and Code: Lightweight Corpus-Grounded Process Supervision for Factual Question Answering
2605.29648.paper_summary: Applying reinforcement learning to improve factual accuracy in knowledge-intensive question answering faces a reward design dilemma. Response-level rewards provide only coarse supervision and cannot distinguish correct from incorrect statements within a reasoning trace. Sentence-level alternatives offer finer-grained feedback, but typically rely on NLI verifiers, LLM judges, or knowledge-verification pipelines that are expensive to deploy at RL scale and often unreliable for rare-entity facts, where accurate reward signals are especially important. We propose CorVer (Corpus Verify), a lightweight, plug-in-ready process reward that replaces neural verifiers with a corpus-grounded signal derived from Wikipedia co-occurrence statistics. CorVer assigns sentence-level credit and maps it to token-level advantages via a simple alignment, requiring only a 0.5B extractor and a single corpus lookup per sentence. Across 30 (model, benchmark) cells spanning six instruction-tuned models (3B to 14B) and five QA benchmarks, CorVer improves over the raw baseline for every cell, with an average TriviaQA gain of +4.1 pp. It also outperforms four neural-verifier baselines in 18 of 20 cells under their feasible configurations, while training 4.8 to 8.4x faster.
2605.29648.paper_summary_zh: 應用增強學習來提高知識密集型問題回答中的事實準確性面臨獎勵設計的困境。回應級別的獎勵僅提供粗略的監督，無法在推理過程中區分正確與不正確的陳述。句子級別的替代方案提供了更細緻的反饋，但通常依賴於NLI驗證器、LLM評判或知識驗證管道，這些在增強學習規模下部署成本高昂，且對於稀有實體事實往往不可靠，而準確的獎勵信號在這些情況下尤其重要。我們提出了CorVer（Corpus Verify），這是一種輕量級的即插即用過程獎勵，通過來自維基百科共現統計的語料庫基礎信號取代神經驗證器。CorVer為句子級別分配信用，並通過簡單的對齊將其映射到令牌級別的優勢，只需每句話0.5B的提取器和一次語料庫查詢。在涵蓋六個指令調整模型（3B到14B）和五個QA基準的30個（模型，基準）單元中，CorVer在每個單元上都超越了原始基線，平均TriviaQA增益為+4.1個百分點。它在20個單元中的18個單元下也超越了四個神經驗證器基線，並且訓練速度快了4.8到8.4倍。
2605.29648.author: Shicheng Fan et.al.
2605.29648.authors: Shicheng Fan, Haochang Hao, Dehai Min, Weihao Liu, Philip S. Yu, Lu Cheng
